sections.label: Section 1
sections.original: Ἑρμῆ χθόνιε , πατρῷ ἐποπτεύων κράτη ,
sections.translation: Hermes of the nether world , you who guard the powers that are your father ’ s , [*] prove yourself my savior and ally , I entreat you , now that I have come to this land and returned from exile . On this mounded grave I cry out to my father

sections.label: Section 5
sections.original: κλύειν , ἀκοῦσαι . . .
sections.translation:
to hearken , to hear me ⋯
Look , I bring a lock to Inachus [*] in requital for his care , and here , a second , in token of my grief .
For I was not present , father , to lament your death , nor did I stretch forth my hand to bear your corpse .

sections.label: Section 10
sections.original: ἔα . [*] [*] τί χρῆμα λεύσσω ; τίς ποθ ἥδ ὁμήγυρις
sections.translation: What is this I see ? What is this throng of women that moves in state , marked by their sable cloaks ? To what calamity should I set this down ? Is it some new sorrow that befalls our house ? Or am I right to suppose that for my father ’ s sake

sections.label: Section 15
sections.original: χοὰς φερούσας νερτέροις μειλίγματα ; [*]
sections.translation: they bear these libations to appease the powers below ? It can only be for this cause : for indeed I think my own sister Electra is approaching , distinguished by her bitter grief . Oh grant me , Zeus , to avenge my father ’ s death , and may you be my willing ally !

sections.label: Section 20
sections.original: Πυλάδη , σταθῶμεν ἐκποδών , [*] ὡς ἂν σαφῶς
sections.translation: Pylades , let us stand apart , that I may know clearly what this band of suppliant women intends . Exit Orestes and Pylades . Enter Electra with women carrying libations .

sections.label: Section 1.22
sections.original: ἰαλτὸς ἐκ δόμων ἔβαν [*]
sections.translation: Sent forth from the palace I have come to convey libations to the sound of sharp blows of my hands . My cheek is marked with bloody gashes

sections.label: Section 1.25
sections.original: ὄνυχος ἄλοκι νεοτόμῳ ,
sections.translation: where my nails have cut fresh furrows . And yet through all my life my heart is fed with lamentation . Rips are torn by my griefs through the linen web of my garment , torn in the cloth that covers my breast ,

sections.label: Section 1.30
sections.original: πέπλων ἀγελάστοις
sections.translation: the cloth of robes struck for the sake of my mirthless misfortunes .

sections.label: Section 1.32
sections.original: τορὸς γὰρ ὀρθόθριξ φόβος , [*]
sections.translation: For with a hair-raising shriek , Terror , the diviner of dreams for our house , breathing wrath out of sleep , uttered a cry of terror in the dead of night from the heart of the palace ,

sections.label: Section 1.35
sections.original: μυχόθεν ἔλακε [*] περὶ φόβῳ ,
sections.translation: a cry that fell heavily on the women ’ s quarter . [*] And the readers of these dreams , bound under pledge , cried out from the god that those

sections.label: Section 1.40
sections.original: νέρθεν περιθύμως
sections.translation: beneath the earth cast furious reproaches and rage against their murderers .

sections.label: Section 2.42
sections.original: τοιάνδε χάριν ἀχάριτον [*] ἀπότροπον κακῶν ,
sections.translation: Intending to ward off evil with such a graceless grace , O mother Earth ,

sections.label: Section 2.45
sections.original: μωμένα μ ἰάλλει [*]
sections.translation: she sends me forth , godless woman that she is . But I am afraid to utter the words she charged me to speak . For what atonement is there for blood fallen to earth ? Ah , hearth of utter grief !

sections.label: Section 2.50
sections.original: ἰὼ κατασκαφαὶ δόμων .
sections.translation: Ah , house laid low in ruin ! Sunless darkness , loathed by men , enshrouds our house due to the death of its master .

sections.label: Section 2.55
sections.original: σέβας δ ἄμαχον ἀδάματον [*] ἀπόλεμον τὸ πρὶν
sections.translation: The awe of majesty once unconquered , unvanquished , irresistible in war , that penetrated the ears and heart of the people , is now cast off . But there is still fear . And prosperity — this ,

sections.label: Section 2.60
sections.original: τόδ ἐν βροτοῖς [*] θεός τε καὶ θεοῦ πλέον .
sections.translation: among mortals , is a god and more than a god . But the balance of Justice keeps watch : swiftly it descends on those in the light ; sometimes pain waits for those who linger on the frontier of twilight ;

sections.label: Section 2.65
sections.original: τοὺς δ ἄκραντος ἔχει νύξ .
sections.translation: and others are claimed by strengthless night .

sections.label: Section 3.66
sections.original: δι αἵματ ἐκποθένθ [*] ὑπὸ χθονὸς τροφοῦ
sections.translation: Because of blood drunk up by the fostering earth , the vengeful gore lies clotted and will not dissolve away . Soul-racking calamity distracts

sections.label: Section 3.70
sections.original: τὸν αἴτιον παναρκέτας νόσου βρύειν .
sections.translation: the guilty man till he is steeped in utter misery .

sections.label: Section 3.71
sections.original: θιγόντι [*] δ οὔτι νυμφικῶν ἑδωλίων
sections.translation: But for the violator of a bridal chamber there is no cure . And though all streams flow in one course to cleanse the blood from a polluted hand , they rush in vain .

sections.label: Section 75
sections.original: ἐμοὶ δ ??? ἀνάγκαν γὰρ ἀμφίπτολιν
sections.translation: For since the gods laid constraining doom about my city and led me from my father ’ s house to a slave ’ s lot , it is fitting for me to govern my bitter hate , even against my will , and submit to the wishes of my masters , whether just or unjust .

sections.label: Section 82
sections.original: ματαίοισι δεσποτᾶν
sections.translation: But I weep beneath my veil over the senseless fate of my lord , my heart chilled by secret grief .

sections.label: Section 85
sections.original: ἐπεὶ πάρεστε τῆσδε προστροπῆς ἐμοὶ
sections.translation: give me your counsel on this : what should I say while I pour these offerings of sorrow ? How shall I find gracious words , how shall I entreat my father ? Shall I say that I bring these offerings to a loved husband from a loving

sections.label: Section 90
sections.original: γυναικὸς ἀνδρί , τῆς ἐμῆς μητρὸς πάρα ;
sections.translation: wife — from my own mother ? I do not have the assurance for that , nor do I know what I should say as I pour this libation onto my father ’ s tomb . Or shall I speak the words that men are accustomed to use : To those who send these honors may he return benefits —

sections.label: Section 95
sections.original: στέφη , δόσιν γε [*] τῶν κακῶν ἐπαξίαν ;
sections.translation:
a gift , indeed , to match their evil ? [*]
Or , in silence and dishonor , even as my father perished , shall I pour them out for the earth to drink and then retrace my steps , like one who carries refuse away from a rite , hurling the vessel from me with averted eyes ?

sections.label: Section 100
sections.original: τῆσδ ἐστὲ βουλῆς , ὦ φίλαι , μεταίτιαι :
sections.translation: In this , my friends , be my fellow-counsellors . For we cherish a common hatred within our house . Do not hide your counsel in your hearts in fear of anyone . For the portion of fate awaits both the free man and the man enslaved by another ’ s hand .

sections.label: Section 105
sections.original: λέγοις ἄν , εἴ τι τῶνδ ἔχοις [*] ὑπέρτερον .
sections.translation: If you have a better course to urge , speak !

sections.label: Section 106
sections.original: αἰδουμένη σοι βωμὸν ὡς τύμβον πατρὸς
sections.translation: In reverence for your father ’ s tomb , as if it were an altar , I will speak my thoughts from the heart , since you command me .

sections.label: Section 108
sections.original: λέγοις ἄν , ὥσπερ ᾐδέσω τάφον πατρός .
sections.translation: Speak , even as you revere my father ’ s grave .

sections.label: Section 109
sections.original: φθέγγου χέουσα κεδνὰ [*] τοῖσιν εὔφροσιν .
sections.translation: While you pour , utter benedictions for loyal hearts .

sections.label: Section 110
sections.original: τίνας δὲ τούτους τῶν φίλων προσεννέπω ;
sections.translation: And to whom of those dear to me should I address them ?

sections.label: Section 111
sections.original: πρῶτον μὲν αὑτὴν χὤστις Αἴγισθον στυγεῖ .
sections.translation: First to yourself , then to whoever hates Aegisthus .

sections.label: Section 112
sections.original: ἐμοί τε καὶ σοί τἄρ ἐπεύξομαι [*] τάδε ;
sections.translation: Then for myself and for you also shall I make this prayer ?

sections.label: Section 113
sections.original: αὐτὴ σὺ ταῦτα μανθάνουσ ἤδη φράσαι .
sections.translation: That is for you , using your judgment , to consider now for yourself .

sections.label: Section 114
sections.original: τίν οὖν ἔτ ἄλλον τῇδε προστιθῶ στάσει ;
sections.translation: Then whom else should I add to our company ?

sections.label: Section 115
sections.original: μέμνησ Ὀρέστου , κεἰ θυραῖός ἐσθ ὅμως .
sections.translation: Remember Orestes , though he is still away from home .

sections.label: Section 116
sections.original: εὖ τοῦτο , κἀφρένωσας οὐχ ἥκιστά με .
sections.translation: Well said ! You have indeed admonished me thoughtfully .

sections.label: Section 117
sections.original: τοῖς αἰτίοις νῦν [*] τοῦ φόνου μεμνημένη ???
sections.translation: For the guilty murderers now , mindful of —

sections.label: Section 118
sections.original: τί φῶ ; δίδασκ ἄπειρον ἐξηγουμένη .
sections.translation: What should I say ? Instruct my inexperience , prescribe the form .

sections.label: Section 119
sections.original: ἐλθεῖν τιν αὐτοῖς δαίμον ἢ βροτῶν τινα ???
sections.translation: Pray that some divinity or some mortal may come to them —

sections.label: Section 120
sections.original: πότερα δικαστὴν ἢ δικηφόρον λέγεις ; [*]
sections.translation: As judge or as avenger , do you mean ?

sections.label: Section 121
sections.original: ἅπλῶς τι φράζουσ , ὅστις ἀνταποκτενεῖ .
sections.translation: Say in plain speech , One who will take life for life .

sections.label: Section 122
sections.original: καὶ ταῦτά μοὐστὶν εὐσεβῆ θεῶν πάρα ;
sections.translation: And is it right for me to ask this of the gods ?

sections.label: Section 123
sections.original: πῶς δ οὐ τὸν ἐχθρὸν ἀνταμείβεσθαι κακοῖς ;
sections.translation: How could it not be right to repay an enemy with ills ?

sections.label: Section 124
sections.original: κῆρυξ μέγιστε [*] τῶν ἄνω τε καὶ κάτω ,
sections.translation: Supreme herald of the realm above and the realm below , O Hermes of the nether world , come to my aid ,

sections.label: Section 125
sections.original: τοὺς γῆς ἔνερθε δαίμονας κλύειν ἐμὰς
sections.translation: summon to me the spirits beneath the earth to hear my prayers , spirits that watch over my father ’ s house , and Earth herself , who gives birth to all things , and having nurtured them receives their increase in turn . And meanwhile , as I pour these lustral offerings to the dead ,

sections.label: Section 130
sections.original: λέγω καλοῦσα πατέρ , “ ἐποίκτιρόν τ ἐμὲ
sections.translation: I invoke my father : Have pity both on me and on dear Orestes ! How shall we rule our own house ? For now we are bartered away like vagrants by her who bore us , by her who in exchange got as her mate Aegisthus , who was her accomplice in your murder .

sections.label: Section 135
sections.original: κἀγὼ μὲν ἀντίδουλος : ἐκ δὲ χρημάτων
sections.translation: As for me , I am no better than a slave , Orestes is an outcast from his inheritance , while they in their insolence revel openly in the winnings of your toil . But that Orestes may come home with good fortune I pray to you , father : Oh , hearken to me !

sections.label: Section 140
sections.original: αὐτῇ τέ μοι δὸς σωφρονεστέραν πολὺ
sections.translation:
And as for myself , grant that I may prove far more circumspect than my mother and more reverent in deed .
I utter these prayers on our behalf , but I ask that your avenger appear to our foes , father , and that your killers may be killed in just retribution .

sections.label: Section 145
sections.original: ταῦτ ἐν μέσῳ τίθημι τῆς καλῆς [*] ἀρᾶς ,
sections.translation:
So I interrupt my prayer for good to offer them this prayer for evil . But be a bearer of blessings for us to the upper world , with the help of the gods and Earth and Justice crowned with victory . She pours out the libations
Such are my prayers , and over them I pour out these libations .

sections.label: Section 150
sections.original: ὑμᾶς δὲ κωκυτοῖς ἐπανθίζειν νόμος ,
sections.translation: It is right for you to crown them with lamentations , raising your voices in a chant for the dead .

sections.label: Section 152
sections.original: ἵετε δάκρυ καναχὲς ὀλόμενον
sections.translation: Pour forth your tears , splashing as they fall for our fallen lord , to accompany this protection against evil , this charm for the good

sections.label: Section 155
sections.original: ἀπότροπον ἄγος [*] ἀπεύχετον
sections.translation:
against the loathsome pollution . Hear me , oh hear me , my honored lord , out of the darkness of your spirit . [*]
Woe , woe , woe ! Oh for

sections.label: Section 160
sections.original: σθενὴς ἀνήρ , ἀναλυτὴρ δόμων ,
sections.translation: a man mighty with the spear to deliver our house , an Ares , brandishing in the fight the springing Scythian bow and wielding his hilted sword in close combat . As they conclude , Electra discovers the lock of Orestes ’ hair

sections.label: Section 164
sections.original: ἔχει μὲν ἤδη γαπότους [*] χοὰς πατήρ : [*]
sections.translation: My father has by now received the libations , which the earth has drunk .

sections.label: Section 165
sections.original: νέου δὲ μύθου τοῦδε κοινωνήσατε .
sections.translation: But take your share of this startling news .

sections.label: Section 166
sections.original: λέγοις ἄν : ὀρχεῖται δὲ καρδία φόβῳ .
sections.translation: Speak — but my heart is dancing with fear .

sections.label: Section 167
sections.original: ὁρῶ [*] τομαῖον τόνδε βόστρυχον τάφῳ .
sections.translation: I see here a lock cut as an offering for the tomb .

sections.label: Section 168
sections.original: τίνος ποτ ἀνδρός , ἢ βαθυζώνου κόρης ;
sections.translation: A man ’ s , or a deep-girt maid ’ s ?

sections.label: Section 170
sections.original: εὐξύμβολον τόδ ἐστὶ παντὶ δοξάσαι .
sections.translation: That is open to conjecture — anyone may guess .
